items.title: GeoMoka Model Documentation
items.text:
GeoMoka Model is a geospatial semantic segmentation framework focused on remote sensing workflows.

This documentation covers:

  • Project setup and installation
  • End-to-end training workflow
  • CLI command reference
  • Codebase structure for contributors

items.title: Highlights
items.text:
• Multiple segmentation backbones via segmentation-models-pytorch
  • DPT/DINOv2 model support
  • Dataset split tooling for segmentation datasets
  • Config-driven training and inference pipeline

items.title: Quick Links
items.text:
• Start here: Getting Started
  • Run experiments: Training Workflow
  • CLI usage: CLI Reference
  • Explore internals: Codebase Overview

items.title: geomoka-train
items.text:
Train a segmentation model from a YAML config.

geomoka-train --config <path/to/config.yaml> [--save_dir output]

Arguments:

  • --config (required): config file path
  • --save_dir (optional): directory for checkpoints/logs (default: output)

items.title: geomoka-split
items.text:
Generate train/val/test split CSVs from image and label folders.

geomoka-split --root <dataset_root> [--out_dir <splits_dir>] [--train_val_test 0.7 0.15 0.15]

Expected under --root:

  • images/
  • labels/

Outputs:

  • train.csv
  • val.csv
  • test.csv
  • unlabeled.csv (only when unmatched images exist)

items.title: geomoka-scaffold
items.text:
Create minimal project directories and copy starter script/test files.

geomoka-scaffold [--root <project_root>]

items.title: geomoka-template
items.text:
Copy template config files into your project.

geomoka-template [--root <project_root>] [--config generic|isprs_postdam] [--force]

Arguments:

  • --config: template set name (generic by default)
  • --force: overwrite existing files

items.title: geomoka.cli.calibrate (module entry)
items.text:
Calibrate confidence threshold to maximize mIoU on validation set.

python -m geomoka.cli.calibrate --config <config.yaml> --checkpoint <model.pth>

items.title: Package Layout
items.text:
Main Python package lives under src/geomoka/.

Key modules:

  • cli/: command-line entrypoints for training, splitting, scaffolding, templating, calibration
  • dataloader/: dataset classes, transforms, mask conversion, wrappers
  • model/: model builders/wrappers and architecture modules (including DINOv2 + DPT)
  • train/: trainer loops and training orchestration
  • eval/: evaluation pipeline and segmentation metrics
  • inference/: inference engines (including rasterio-based paths)
  • losses/: custom losses such as OHEM
  • util/: distributed and general helper utilities

items.title: Configuration and Templates
items.text:
• Runtime configs: config/
  • Built-in templates: src/geomoka/template/config/

Template command copies these into project config/<name>/.

items.title: Data and Outputs
items.text:
• Dataset storage: dataset/
  • Experiment outputs/checkpoints: output/
  • Utility scripts: script/

items.title: Tests and Experiments
items.text:
• Notebook experiments are under test/ and src/geomoka/template/test/

When adding new modules, prefer keeping the same separation:

  • CLI + config at boundaries
  • Training/inference orchestration in dedicated subpackages
  • Reusable pure components in model/, dataloader/, eval/, losses/

items.title: Requirements
items.text:
• Python 3.9+
  • Linux/macOS/Windows environment with PyTorch support

items.title: Installation
items.text:
From the project root:

pip install -r requirements.txt
pip install -e .

items.title: Project Bootstrap
items.text:
Create base folders for a new project workspace:

geomoka-scaffold --root .

Create starter configuration files:

geomoka-template --root . --config generic

Available config templates:

  • generic
  • isprs_postdam

items.title: Dataset Layout
items.text:
Expected segmentation dataset structure:

dataset/
  <dataset-name>/
    images/
    labels/
    splits/
      train.csv
      val.csv
      test.csv

Use the split CLI to generate CSV splits:

geomoka-split --root dataset/<dataset-name>

items.title: Build and Serve Documentation
items.text:
Install docs dependencies:

pip install -e .[docs]

Run local docs server:

mkdocs serve

Build static docs:

mkdocs build

items.title: Headers
items.text:
# H1 Header
## H2 Header
### H3 Header
#### H4 Header
##### H5 Header
###### H6 Header

items.title: Text formatting
items.text:
**bold text**
*italic text*
***bold and italic***
~~strikethrough~~
`inline code`

items.title: Links and images
items.text:
[Link text](https://example.com)
[Link with title](https://example.com "Hover title")
![Alt text](image.jpg)
![Image with title](image.jpg "Image title")

items.title: Lists
items.text:
Unordered:
- Item 1
- Item 2
  - Nested item

Ordered:
1. First item
2. Second item
3. Third item

items.title: Blockquotes
items.text:
> This is a blockquote
> Multiple lines
>> Nested quote

items.title: Code blocks
items.text:
```javascript
function hello() {
  console.log("Hello, world!");
}
```

items.title: Tables
items.text:
| Header 1 | Header 2 | Header 3 |
|----------|----------|----------|
| Row 1    | Data     | Data     |
| Row 2    | Data     | Data     |

items.title: Horizontal rule
items.text:
---
or
***
or
___

items.title: Task lists
items.text:
- [x] Completed task
- [ ] Incomplete task
- [ ] Another task

items.title: Escaping characters
items.text: Use backslash to escape: \* \_ \# \`

items.title: Line breaks
items.text:
End a line with two spaces  
to create a line break.

Or use a blank line for a new paragraph.

items.title: 1) Prepare Configuration
items.text:
Training is driven by YAML config files under config/.

Typical workflow:

  1. Generate template configs with geomoka-template
  2. Update dataset paths and split CSV paths
  3. Select model architecture and backbone
  4. Tune training hyperparameters

items.title: 2) Train Model
items.text:
Run training with a config file:

geomoka-train --config config/isprs_postdam/train_dpt.yaml --save_dir output

--save_dir stores checkpoints and logs.

items.title: 3) Validate and Evaluate
items.text:
Validation/test behavior depends on the trainer/evaluation settings inside your config.

Evaluation utilities are implemented in:

  • src/geomoka/eval/evaluate.py
  • src/geomoka/eval/metrics.py

items.title: 4) Confidence Threshold Calibration
items.text:
Use the calibration CLI to tune prediction confidence threshold on validation data:

python -m geomoka.cli.calibrate \
  --config config/isprs_postdam/train_dpt.yaml \
  --checkpoint output/<run>/best_model.pth \
  --mode sliding_window \
  --output output/<run>/confidence_calibration.png

This command saves:

  • Calibration curve image
  • JSON summary with best threshold and mIoU curve
